channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DHS seeks to deputize IRS agents to help with deportation efforts
item.description: Trump has reportedly been increasingly frustrated with the number of immigration arrests since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth renames North Carolina military base Fort Roland L. Bragg
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has signed an order restoring the name of a storied special operations forces base back to Fort Bragg.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump names Grenell as interim Kennedy Center executive director
item.description: President Trump on Monday named Richard Grenell, his envoy for special missions, the interim executive director of the Kennedy Center days after he named himself chair of a new board. “I am pleased to announce that Ric Grenell will serve as the Interim Executive Director of The Kennedy Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders fired head of whistleblower agency reinstated while fight continues over Trump removal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A U.S. judge on Monday ordered the fired head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers to be reinstated while a court fight continues over his removal by President Donald Trump. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace accuses ex-fiancé and associates of assaulting her and raping others in House speech
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina is raising allegations against a former fiancé and his business associates.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration targets Education Department research arm in latest cuts
item.description: The Institute of Education Sciences (IES) is responsible for gathering data on a wide range of topics, including research-backed teaching practices and the state of U.S. student achievement.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta and Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s ban on gender-affirming care for young people puts hospitals in a bind
item.description: Hospitals and clinics that have offered gender-affirming treatments to transgender youth reacted in a variety of ways to an executive order that aims to halt the care.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: US cyber agency puts election security staffers who worked with the states on leave
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Staffers at the nation’s cybersecurity agency whose job is to ensure the security of U.S. elections have been placed on administrative leave, jeopardizing critical support provided to state and local election offices across the country. In recent days, 17 employees of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Ohio State coach Jim Tressel joins a long list of sports figures who've moved into politics
item.description: College Football Hall of Fame coach Jim Tressel, who won the 2002 national title at Ohio State University, has been nominated to be the state’s next lieutenant governor, joining a long list of sports figures who’ve moved into politics.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from longstanding norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Secrecy preceded the shutdown of the consumer protection agency's Washington headquarters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the weekend, some staff members at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau saw a sign of trouble to come. Windows in two basement conference rooms were covered with brown paper and blue painter's tape, concealing their occupants.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress now has a DOGE Caucus. Here's how its co-chair says it will work
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Rep. Pete Sessions, co-chair of the House DOGE Caucus, on how he plans to work with the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Kai McNamee, and Justine Kenin, MPR News

item.title: Fox News Politics News Letter: Ex-college football coach tapped by governor
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Trump administration has stopped work at the CFPB. Here's what the agency does
item.description: It's the main U.S. agency tasked with overseeing the financial products and services used by everyday Americans. The CFPB's new head has closed its offices and told staff to stay home.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: NY Democrats blink as controversial state election bill affecting Rep. Stefanik seat declared dead: reports
item.description: The bill sought to give Gov. Kathy Hochul the power to merge election dates, leading minority party lawmakers like New York state Sen. Rob Ortt to rail against it as an affront to voters in the rural, red North Country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. judge keeps his block on Trump federal buyout plan in place for now
item.description: A U.S. judge kept his block on President Donald Trump’s buyout plan for federal employees in place on Monday and said he would consider imposing it for a longer period of time.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing. Much better," Trump told Fox News's Bret Baier in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'Fork in the Road' resignation offer for federal workers is in judge's hands
item.description: After a hearing on Monday, a federal judge in Boston extended a stay on the deadline for federal employees to accept the Trump administration's resignation offer while he considers the arguments.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Schumer warns of 'Trump shutdown,' lays out 4-pronged plan for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) in a letter to colleagues Monday warned of the possibility of a “Trump shutdown” and reminded fellow senators that Democrats have the power to make or break any bill to fund the government past March 14.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kick start arrives for Minnesota lawmakers after standoff in session’s opening weeks
item.description: A push to roll out bills and organize committees comes after the House got started last week and the Senate ended a power-sharing deal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Elon Musk as 'terrific' during Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump calls federal courts blocking Elon Musk "crazy," and predicted the Kansas City Chiefs would win Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Musk question the authority of the courts as Trump's agenda faces legal pushback
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top Trump administration officials are openly questioning the judiciary's authority to serve as a check on executive power as the new president's sweeping agenda faces growing pushback from the courts.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about presidents and security clearances
item.description: Both former President Joe Biden and President Donald Trump have barred their predecessor access to classified information. But historically, it wasn't like that.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor's Race Poll: Cuomo the favorite, Adams looking stronger
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An exclusive poll from PIX11 News, Emerson College, and The Hill shows former Gov. Andrew Cuomo would hold an early advantage if he hops in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary — but Mayor Eric Adams still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
